name: North Waterfront
description: The storied northeast waterfront: Italian espresso bars, Beat-era bookshops, wild parrots, and the ever-busy Pier 39.

name: Marina & Pacific Heights
description: Victorian mansions, boutique dining strips, and a front-row seat to the Bay.

name: Presidio & Richmond
description: Ancient forests, Clement Street dim sum, and the quieter side of the peninsula.

name: Russian Hill & Nob Hill
description: Cable cars, crooked streets, Chinatown dim sum, and the city's steepest views.

name: Downtown & Embarcadero
description: The Ferry Building, the Salesforce tower, and the city's commercial and civic heartbeat.

name: Civic Center & Hayes Valley
description: City Hall, the symphony, painted ladies, and a bustling arts-and-restaurant corridor.

name: SoMa & Mission Bay
description: Tech campuses, Oracle Park, converted warehouses, and the city's fastest-changing waterfront.

name: Haight & Cole Valley
description: The original Summer of Love neighborhood: record shops, vintage stores, and Golden Gate Park at the doorstep.

name: Castro & Noe Valley
description: One of the world's most celebrated LGBTQ+ communities, plus sunny Noe Valley's stroller-friendly 24th Street.

name: Mission & Bernal Heights
description: Murals, taquerias, tech workers, and longtime Latino families — the Mission's contradictions are its vitality.

name: The Sunset
description: Fog, surf, N Judah streetcars, and Irving Street restaurants stretching all the way to the ocean.

name: Bayview & Excelsior
description: The city's largest and most diverse cluster — working-class communities, new development, and deep roots.
